L1.area: Delegation metadata resolution and target update retrieval logic

L2.description: A race condition vulnerability exists where delegation role lookups use an unordered data structure, causing non-deterministic traversal order of delegated roles. This can result in the wrong artifact being selected when multiple delegations exist, as the iteration order varies unpredictably between executions.

L3.area: Delegation role resolution within the TUF metadata package and the target updater's delegation graph traversal
L3.description: A race condition vulnerability exists where delegation role lookups for target files use an unordered map data structure instead of an ordered list, causing non-deterministic traversal order during the graph walk of delegated roles. When multiple delegations exist in a TUF repository's target metadata, the inconsistent ordering can result in the wrong artifact being downloaded.

ground_truth.reason: The GetRolesForTarget function returns a Go map, whose iteration order is non-deterministic, causing delegation roles to be traversed in an inconsistent order that can lead to downloading the wrong artifact.
